_risk_level_doc: Modifies a PreToolUse security hook. Change is additive (new allow path); no existing block rules are removed. Risk contained to hook file.

requirement.original: When a user uses /allow <command>, agents must be able to execute that command — including commands that currently hit the ref-mutation hard wall (e.g. git branch -D, git worktree remove --force). No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.
requirement.clarified: pretool-git-privilege-guard.py must honor structured sentinel grants written by /allow, including in subagent context. The sentinel grant check must be added to _check_git_allowlist() and must not be suppressed by the subagent firewall.
requirement.what: Add match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() check to _check_git_allowlist() in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
requirement.why: The function only checks the legacy grant file and never sees the structured sentinel grant that /allow writes. This makes /allow silently fail for git ref-mutation commands even when the user has explicitly granted them.
success_criteria: After /allow git branch -D foo, an agent can run git branch -D foo without BLOCKED output from pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
Subagent firewall does NOT suppress the sentinel grant check (subagents can use /allow-granted commands)
No existing block rules are weakened — only the sentinel grant path is added
constraints: Do not alter the force-push ordering in _evaluate_push() — _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation() must remain BEFORE _check_git_allowlist()
Subagent firewall for LEGACY grant path (read_grant_for_git_command) must remain unchanged
task_id resolution must use CLAUDE_TASK_ID env var with SID fallback, matching userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:183

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/allowlist.py (read-only — match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is imported, not changed)
out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (already correct — sentinel exits 0 at line 521 before reaching ref-mutation block at line 1015)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: git worktree remove --force is not matched by any pattern in pretool-bash-safety.sh or pretool-git-privilege-guard.py. User cited it as an example of a blocked command, but it is not blocked by these hooks. If it IS blocked, the blocking must be in a different hook not examined in this cycle.

Free-text /allow inputs (e.g. /allow 你自己收尾) produce a sentinel with op equal to the entire free-text token. This never matches any bash subcommand's first token. Silent failure — user gets a GRANTED message but the sentinel never fires. Separate usability ticket.

&&-chained commands: match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() checks each subcommand independently. Only ONE sentinel entry is matched; later sub-commands in the chain are checked by their own sentinel entries. If the user wrote /allow git branch -D foo and the command is git status && git branch -D foo, only one of the two subcommands needs to match — which is correct. However if the user wants to allow a specific compound command, they must /allow each sub-command or use a regex. Separate usability ticket.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: After /allow git branch -D foo, running git branch -D foo is still blocked with BLOCKED: agent direct git ref mutation from pretool-git-privilege-guard.py
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: _check_git_allowlist() in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py calls only read_grant_for_git_command() (legacy grant). It never calls match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command(). The structured sentinel grant written by /allow is invisible to the privilege guard.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — structural omission, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The sentinel grant system (task 20260519-211515 R2) was added to bash-safety.sh. The privilege guard was not updated to consume sentinel grants at the same time.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Two parallel PreToolUse hooks both run for every Bash call. A single hook emitting 'allow' is sufficient to permit the tool, BUT bash-safety.sh consumes the sentinel grant atomically. If bash-safety.sh exits 0 (sentinel matched), the privilege guard still runs independently and blocks on its own check — because from its perspective no grant was found.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Sentinel system added ~2026-05-19. Privilege guard not updated at that time.
observations: Read hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:144-155 — _check_git_allowlist() does not call match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() (inferred from source read)
Read hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:496-521 — sentinel check exits 0 before line 1015 (inferred from source read)

Codex invocation for this cycle blocked by bash-safety.sh ref-mutation pattern matching on prompt text containing git branch -D — observed runtime behavior confirming the block fires on any command text containing these patterns
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete — inferred from source read; runtime reproduction not performed (hook fires on command text, making direct reproduction from within a hook-gated shell session circular)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Source read of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:144-155 shows _check_git_allowlist() calls only read_grant_for_git_command(). Source read of allowlist.py:179-192 shows read_grant_for_git_command() reads /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json (legacy file). Source read of allowlist.py:438-481 shows match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() reads /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json (sentinel file). The two functions read different files; _check_git_allowlist() only calls the legacy one.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: structural omission, not a regression — no commit introduced a break; the sentinel system was added to bash-safety.sh without a corresponding update to the privilege guard

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: When a user uses /allow <command>, agents must be able to execute that command
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: GIVEN a valid sentinel grant for git branch -D foo, WHEN an agent runs git branch -D foo, THEN privilege guard allows it (exit 0)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: including commands that currently hit the ref-mutation hard wall (e.g. git branch -D)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: GIVEN a sentinel for git branch -D foo, WHEN _check_git_allowlist() is called, THEN match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is consulted and returns a match

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: GIVEN a sentinel created by /allow, WHEN any agent (subagent or main-agent) runs the allowed command, THEN the subagent firewall does NOT suppress the sentinel check

component_chain.role: Writes sentinel grant to /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json on /allow command
_check_git_allowlist() — only reads legacy grant, must be extended to read sentinel
match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() — structural match of bash command against sentinel grant

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Subagent firewall — prevents subagents from bypassing git privilege guard via legacy /allow grant
Force-push must be blocked even with a broad /allow grant — checked BEFORE _check_git_allowlist() in _evaluate_push()

evidence.observed: agents must be able to execute /allow-granted commands including git branch -D

measured.value: _check_git_allowlist() at lines 144-155 calls only read_grant_for_git_command(); match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() is absent

expected.value: _check_git_allowlist() must also call match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() — consistent with bash-safety.sh sentinel handling at lines 496-521

evidence.gap: match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() call absent from _check_git_allowlist(); subagent firewall unconditionally suppresses all grant checks for subagents

reference_source.description: bash-safety.sh M2 decision (task 20260521-090200) for subagent sentinel extension; allowlist.py match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() signature

novelty_check.rationale: First attempt — no prior cycle for this issue

development_approach.strategy: Additive change: extend _check_git_allowlist() to also check sentinel grant before returning False. Move the subagent firewall to apply only to the legacy grant path. Add import of match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command at module level.
development_approach.validation_approach: pytest tests in hooks/tests/ — write fixtures that (1) write a sentinel grant file, (2) call _check_git_allowlist() directly, (3) assert True for matching commands; (4) assert False when no grant exists; (5) assert True for subagent context with sentinel.

research_findings.reason: straightforward structural omission — the fix is to add one function call that mirrors an existing pattern in the same codebase

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation was blocked by the very bug being analyzed: pretool-bash-safety.sh's ref-mutation pattern matched on the prompt text containing 'git branch -D'. Exit code 2, BLOCKED by hook. This is direct observed evidence of the bash-safety.sh pattern-matching behavior on command strings. Self-review substituted.

findings.issue: Codex prompt was rejected by hook because prompt text contained git branch -D as a substring — the hook pattern-matched on the PROMPT env var content, not on an executable git command
findings.rationale: This is an interesting behavior of the hook but it is out-of-scope for this ticket. The hook correctly fires on any Bash command line containing the ref-mutation pattern strings, even when those strings appear in argument values like PROMPT=... rather than as executable git commands. Separate observation.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — five adversarial questions reviewed: (1) root cause attribution to privilege guard confirmed correct; (2) sentinel check in _check_git_allowlist() confirmed as minimal fix; (3) subagent firewall should NOT apply to sentinel per M2 design; (4) git worktree remove --force confirmed not blocked by either hook; (5) no scope drift found
